title: Static Delivery, HTTP Representations, URLs, Bases, and Origins · Program Brief: Static Request Model
summary: Pages work from a local file but break below a project subpath, directories redirect unexpectedly, fragments point nowhere, and the team confuses filesystem paths with URLs. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the static request model increment in versioned URL and delivery contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-ca…
objectives: Trace a browser request through URL resolution, origin, path mapping, representation selection, cache, and response metadata.
Separate parser, renderer, file-tree, browser, accessibility, deployment, cache, and stakeholder evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-http-url-model-4
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-http-url-model-4.
# Case 1655: an accessibility-led community newsroom release.
# Operating constraint: reconcile content, routes, navigation, links, assets, feeds, sitemaps, output files, and artifact digests before approval.
# Changed case: delete a source and change a shared layout, then compare incremental and clean artifact digests.
def worked_static_site_http_url_model_4(requests: int = 7, mapped_routes: int = 7, base_path: str = "/handbook/", broken_fragments: int = 0, media_types_valid: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "readerworked4",
        "source1655",
        "parse4dekrow",
        "routeworked41655",
        "artifact1655worked4",
        "recovery4dekrow1655",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if requests < 1 or mapped_routes < 1:
        return False, mapped_routes, "request-shape"
    if mapped_routes != requests:
        return False, mapped_routes, "route-mapping"
    if not base_path.startswith("/") or not base_path.endswith("/"):
        return False, mapped_routes, "base-path"
    if broken_fragments > 0 or not media_types_valid:
        return False, broken_fragments, "delivery-contract"
    assert mapped_routes == requests
    return True, mapped_routes, "resolved-static-delivery"
content.caption: Runnable deterministic pure-Python static-site decision model; trace one accepted and one rejected source, parse, route, render, asset, accessibility, cache, deployment, or recovery case, then state which filesystem, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validator, browser, GitHub, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, or production claim still needs controlled transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-static-request-model-1n40ey
# Competency: ssg-static-request-model.
# Case 5380: a multilingual public-service handbook migration.
# Operating constraint: test empty, malformed, deeply nested, Unicode, collision, unsafe URL, missing asset, stale cache, rejected, and recovery cases.
# Changed case: zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks.
def evidence_ssg_static_request_model_1n40ey(requests: int = 7, mapped_routes: int = 7, base_path: str = "/handbook/", broken_fragments: int = 0, media_types_valid: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1n40ey = "1n40ey-ye04n1-5380"
    assert evidence_variant_1n40ey
    evidence_axes_1n40ey = (
        "reader1n40ey",
        "source5380",
        "parseye04n1",
        "route1n40ey5380",
        "artifact53801n40ey",
        "recoveryye04n15380",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1n40ey)) == 6
    if requests < 1 or mapped_routes < 1:
        return False, mapped_routes, "request-shape"
    if mapped_routes != requests:
        return False, mapped_routes, "route-mapping"
    if not base_path.startswith("/") or not base_path.endswith("/"):
        return False, mapped_routes, "base-path"
    if broken_fragments > 0 or not media_types_valid:
        return False, broken_fragments, "delivery-contract"
    assert mapped_routes == requests
    return True, mapped_routes, "resolved-static-delivery"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Static Request Model model · Theory Static Request Mod…
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Static Request Model: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory publishing team handles case 7280 during a large technical reference incremental rebuild. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile static requests, base-path URL resolution, routes, fragments, and representation metadata; retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity; then zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Trace a browser request through URL resolution, origin, path mapping, representation selection, cache, and response metadata.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Static Request Model model · Theory Static Request Mod….
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Static Request Model model · Theory Static Request Mod…, keep Static Request Model connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Trace a browser request through URL resolution, origin, path mapping, representation selection, cache, and response metadata.

hints: For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Static Request Model model · Theory Static Request Mod…, name the reader, content authority, revision, source, span, parser, trust, route, render, asset, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery boundary, Static Request Model precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Static Request Model model · Theory Static Request Mod…, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, tokenization, parsing, transformation, routing, rendering, validation, publication, failure, rollback, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory publishing team handles case 7280 during a large technical reference incremental rebuild. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile static requests, base-path URL resolution, routes, fragments, and representation metadata; retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity; then zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Trace a browser request through URL resolution, origin, path mapping, representation selection, cache, and response metadata.

For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Static Request Model model · Theory Static Request Mod…, challenge the documented misconception with changed-source, delimiter, Unicode, nesting, route, URL, asset, viewport, cache, security, deployment, recovery, and controlled production-transfer evidence: Static hosting means the browser reads repository files directly.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: ssg-static-request-model-1n40ey.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: ssg-static-request-model-1n40ey(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_ssg_static_request_model_1n40ey\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?requests < 1 or mapped_routes < 1)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?mapped_routes != requests)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not base_path\.startswith\("/"\) or not base_path\.endswith\("/"\))(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?broken_fragments > 0 or not media_types_valid)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
